description: A classic poem matched to the current season and mood

content.prompt_template:
You are a classical literature expert. Based on the environment info, recommend a short poem or excerpt (4-8 lines) from English or Western poetry that resonates with the current season, weather, or mood.
Output JSON: {{"title": "Poem title", "author": "Poet name", "lines": ["line 1", "line 2", ...], "note": "One-line appreciation (under 40 chars)"}}
Requirements:
1. Choose from classic poets: Shakespeare, Frost, Dickinson, Keats, Shelley, Wordsworth, Whitman, Yeats, Rilke, Neruda, etc.
2. Different poem each time
3. Each line as a separate array element
4. All text must be in English
5. Output JSON only
Context: {context}

default: Two roads diverged in a yellow wood,
And sorry I could not travel both
And be one traveler, long I stood
And looked down one as far as I could

note.default: Every choice shapes who we become.

fallback.title: The Road Not Taken
fallback.author: Robert Frost
lines: Two roads diverged in a yellow wood,
And sorry I could not travel both
And be one traveler, long I stood
And looked down one as far as I could
fallback.note: Every choice shapes who we become.

fallback_pool.title: The Road Not Taken
fallback_pool.author: Robert Frost
lines: Two roads diverged in a yellow wood,
And sorry I could not travel both
And be one traveler, long I stood
And looked down one as far as I could
fallback_pool.note: Every choice shapes who we become.

fallback_pool.title: Sonnet 18
fallback_pool.author: William Shakespeare
lines: Shall I compare thee to a summer's day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate.
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of May,
And summer's lease hath all too short a date.

fallback_pool.title: Hope is the thing with feathers
fallback_pool.author: Emily Dickinson
lines: Hope is the thing with feathers
And sings the tune without the words,
